title: Project Report: Production ML Platform with Monitoring — ML / AI Engineer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Batch scoring pipeline with drift detection, model registry, and automated retraining trigger.
objectives: Retrain trigger when drift exceeds threshold
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**Production ML Platform with Monitoring** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for ML / AI Engineer. Duration: 5–6 weeks. Batch scoring pipeline with drift detection, model registry, and automated retraining trigger. Tools: MLflow, Evidently, Airflow, scikit-learn.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Scheduled batch scoring job
Objective: Evidently drift detection report
Objective: Model registry with versioning
Objective: Retrain trigger when drift exceeds threshold
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
ML / AI Engineer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a ML / AI Engineer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a ML / AI Engineer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Batch
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Batch** *(Level: Advanced)*

Scoring pipeline. Complete these tasks: Feature store read; Batch predict. Deliverable for this phase: Daily scores table.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Feature store read
- [ ] Batch predict
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Batch is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it

### Where to use it

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Daily scores table to the teacher.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Batch pipeline; Drift reports; Registry setup; Ops runbook. Skills demonstrated: MLOps, Drift detection, Automation. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Batch pipeline
Deliverable: Drift reports
Deliverable: Registry setup
Deliverable: Ops runbook
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Batch pipeline and passes the rubric.
